DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT COMMONWEALTH LAND TITLE COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to 4th and 6th causes of action as arguments are based on the merits and are not proper for demurrer. A split of authority exists as to whether unjust enrichment exists as a separate cause of action. Overruled as to 10th cause of action. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to remaining causes of action. First cause of action (breach of contract) contains uncertain and inconsistent allegations regarding the oral contract. The second agreement must state the terms with respect to CLTC and state whether written or oral. The 2nd cause of action (breach of implied covenant) is dependent upon 1st cause of action. Third cause of action (B&P Code section 17200) must distinguish the defendants and allege conduct specific to each. As to 5th cause of action (intentional misrepresentation) and 7th cause of action (fraudulent concealment) plaintiff must plead with specificity: who, what, when, and by whom. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
